name: Gumbo Des Herbes
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash all greens thoroughly to remove grit and discard any bad leaves. Place in a large pot with onion and garlic. Cover with water and bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes. Drain the cooked greens, reserving the liquid.
3. Puree the greens in a food processor or grind them in a meat grinder. Set aside.
4. Meanwhile, cut the meats and sausages into bite-sized pieces. Place smoked sausage, ham, stew meat, and brisket in a 12-quart stock pot. Add two cups of the reserved liquid from the greens. Simmer for 15 minutes.
5. Place chouriço in a skillet and simmer until all grease is cooked out. Remove the chouriço from the skillet and set aside.
6. Stir flour into the chouriço drippings in the skillet and cook for five minutes, or until the flour is cooked (it does not have to brown).
7. Pour the roux over the meat in the stock pot, stirring well. Add the pureed greens and two quarts of the reserved liquid from the greens. Simmer for 20 minutes.
8. Add the chouriço, salt, cayenne pepper, and thyme. Stir well and simmer for an additional 40 minutes.
9. Stir in the file powder and remove from heat. Serve over rice. Makes eight servings.
From Chef John Folse of Lafitte's Landing in Donaldsonville, Louisiana
From: Bill Birner Date: 08 Mar 94
